question.text: Under Hindu Succession Act 1956, a daughter has:

answer.text: Equal coparcenary rights to ancestral property as a son
Rights only if mentioned in the Will
Rights only to the mother's property

explanation.text: **The Logic:** After the 2005 amendment to the Hindu Succession Act, daughters have equal coparcenary rights to ancestral property as sons, including the right to claim partition. **The Trap:** Option A was true before the 2005 amendment but is no longer valid. Option C confuses testamentary succession with statutory rights. **Cross-Exam Context:** Hindu Succession Act amendments are covered in Series X-B (estate planning) and are important for retirement advisers under Series XVII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Advisers must be aware of daughters' equal rights when helping clients plan asset distribution. Not accounting for this can lead to legal challenges to the Will.
